name: Best Grilled Burgers
directions: Mix all ingredients and shape into burgers.
Grill to desired doneness.
Top with a slice of your favorite cheese.
We especially like provolone or swiss.
Note: You can add oregano if you like Italian, or taco seasoning if you lie Tex-Mex!

name: Hamburger Gravy My Way
directions: In a large frying pan, brown hamburger, crumbling as you go.
Add minced onion and cook until translucent.
Sprinkle flour over cooked beef, and stir thoroughly.
Cook for 1-2 minutes.
Add 2 cups milk, stir well and add all seasonings at this point.
Simmer, adding more liquid as gravy thickens.
Cook to desired consistancy, and serve over any style potatoes, pasta, rice, or toast.

name: Hamburger Steak and Gravy
directions: Mix ground beef, eggs, bread crumbs, garlic, salt, pepper and onion.
Pat out 4 patties.
Using a wooden spoon handle and make a hole in the center of the patty.
Fry patties over medium-high heat until well done.
Drain grease.
In a separate pan heat cream of mushroom soup, water and package of brown gravy until smooth.
Pour gravy over patties and simmer covered for about 10 minutes.

name: My Favorite Burgers
directions: Preheat a gas grill to 500 degrees (high).
In a glass bowl, combine meat, steak seasoning, and Worcestershire sauce with your hands until well mixed.
Divide and form into four patties and place on the grill on the lowest grill rack.  With the grill lid open, cook four minutes per side.
For the last two minutes, put cheese on the burgers, put buns on the warming rack, and close the lid.
Serve on the buns (duh).

name: Hamburger Homestyle
directions: Throw all the ingredients into a bowl and mix up.
Shape into little balls (Like a golf ball).
Fry in a little butter until cooked.
Serve with salad, bread, peppers and sweetcorn.

name: Bit of Everything Burgers
directions: Mix the beef, egg, bread crumbs, lemon juice, seasonings, pepper, onion and cheese.
Tear some foil pieces large enough to fold in half, but the burger on, then wrap tightly and seal.
Shape 2 patties and place one on each piece of foil. Place onion, potato and carrot slices on top. Wrap the foil tightly around the meat and vegetables.
Bake at 350 for 45 minutes or cook over coals.

name: Burgers - Tasty, Plain and Simple
directions: Combine all ingredients together thoroughly with your hands.
Divide into 4 equal portions. Shape into burgers. *I like to use a burger press
Grill to your liking.
NOTE: Best if assembled a minimum of 60 minutes ahead of time so that flavors have a chance to blend.
Serve with your favorite condiments and toast your buns!

name: Chicken-Fried Hamburger
directions: In a medium bowl, mix together the ground beef, parsley, chili powder, salt, egg, and 1 cup of crushed saltine crackers. Form into patties. Coat patties with remaining cracker crumbs, and place them on a plate. Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Fry patties for about 7 minutes per side, or until the centers are well done and the outside is golden brown.

name: Classic Hamburger
directions: place beef into a large mixing bowl.
dice onion and add to mixture.
add remaining ingredients and mix thouroughly.
make medium sized balls and place on one side of plastic wrap layoug out 4 balls per length of wrap.
fold other half of wrap over top of the ball and press flat.
fold four patties and freeze or cook immediately.

name: Versatile Beef Burgers
directions: Brown ground beef and onion.
Add remaining ingredients and simmer 20 minutes.
Serve on hamburger buns or roll up in corn tortillas with a little shredded cheese and chopped lettuce.

name: Basic Burgers
directions: In bowl, beat egg with water;
Stir in bread crumbs, onion, mustard, garlic, salt and pepper.
Add beef; mix just until combined.
Shape into four 3/4-inch (2 cm) thick patties.
(Make-ahead: Wrap, separated by waxed paper, and refrigerate for up to 24 hours or freeze for up to 1 month; thaw in refrigerator.)
Place on greased grill over medium heat; close lid and grill, turning once, until no longer pink inside and digital thermometer inserted sideways into centre reads 160°F (71°C), about 15 minutes.

name: Hamburger Patties Smothered in Gravy
directions: Mix 2 tsp of the brown gravy with the rest of the patty ingredients then form into 2 inch circles. Brown the patties in a skillet on medium until cooked through.
Mix the ingredients together for the gravy, stirring well. Pour over the cooked patties then cover with a lid & simmer on low for 7 minutes.
Serve hot over mashed potatoes.

name: Ranch Burgers
directions: Preheat the grill to high heat.
In a bowl, mix the ground beef, ranch dressing mix, egg, seasoned bread crumbs, and onion.
Form mix in to 1/4 patties.
Lightly oil the grill grate.  Place patties on the grill, and cook burgers until 165 degrees.
